title: SEO Client Lifecycle
subtitle: Map every stage. Find the breakpoints. Spot the automation.

milestones.name: Discovery & Pitch
milestones.description: From first call to signed SOW: discovery, diagnostic audit, tiered pitch.
milestones.narrative: Sales runs the first discovery call. SEO leadership joins by call two to do a technical and competitive diagnostic. The working team builds a pitch deck and a diagnostic audit. The diagnostic is grounded in crawl data, Ahrefs, and DataForSEO, and the pitch deck is structured around tiered retainer or a la carte packages.
milestones.positioning: The diagnostic audit is the credibility anchor and the pitch deck is the commercial ask. Sequencing risk is real — a savvy client can read the audit and conclude they can DIY the highest-ROI items. Make agency value explicit around things the client cannot easily do internally: link outreach velocity, Reddit operations, AEO citation tracking, programmatic content. Tech fixes get framed as table stakes that unlock everything else.

deliverables: 1-3 key data pulls (brand radar, kw report, reddit crawl) (SEO)
handoffs: Sales hands off call recording, notes, and meeting invite to SEO
SEO creates deck, works on pricing, hands off to full team in Slack channel
After pitch, Sales should follow up
questions: If we have a better discovery deck with more value, how involved does a pitch need to be?
Who should own which part - how do future hires factor in? What is ideal state?
breakpoints: Clear ownership murky - service teams owning most of the prep, deck creation, and pitch itself.
Follow-up ownership murky - varies between Sales/SEO

milestones.name: Kickoff & Onboarding
milestones.description: Access setup, brand guardrails, approval workflows, baseline KPIs.
milestones.narrative: Account lead runs the kickoff call. Kickoff questions focus on access dependencies, messaging guardrails, approval workflows, and escalation paths — not re-covering pitch ground.
milestones.positioning: The first month is where sequencing risk shows up. Plans that front-load posts or technical work in Week 1 assume fast client response, which never happens. The fix is to flag sequencing dependencies in the kickoff itself so client-side blockers do not become agency-side scope problems later.

deliverables: Brand guidelines, content guidelines, etc shared with SEO
activities: (If necessary) Dev meeting for pSEO and technical site work
(If necessary) Content meeting/Legal meeting to align on brand and content guidelines
breakpoints: Not a lot of clear alignment from the start of the engagement about timeline expectations - can we make this more visible?
Make sure that month 1 expectations are set correctly so clients aren't starting off disappointed

milestones.name: Month 1 Execution
milestones.description: Two parallel tracks: technical foundations and visibility quick wins.
milestones.narrative: Two parallel tracks. The technical/foundations track owns site-level fixes that have to ship before content investment pays off. The visibility/quick wins track owns the things the client can see and feel inside 30 days.
milestones.positioning: The existence of a [SHARED] KPI projections file from Month 1 is what makes the QBR achievable. Without that baseline, the QBR becomes a vibes check instead of a measured review. Make this a standing onboarding requirement, not a per-account decision.

goals: Build/maintain strong client trust and communication
Establish brand/content norms - initial feedback put into action
Establish ways of working and comms
kpis: Initial positive bump on anything published
deliverables: Confirmation of strategy after getting all applicable access
handoffs: Initial body of work handed off to clients for feedback

milestones.name: Steady-State Execution
milestones.description: Monthly rhythm: weekly calls, async Slack, monthly deliverables and reporting.
milestones.narrative: The bulk of the engagement. Weekly client status call (account lead + 1-2 ICs), async Slack channel for day-to-day, monthly deliverable cadence per the SOW, and monthly reporting against Good/Better/Best scenarios with a forward-look on next month's priorities.
milestones.positioning: The steady-state phase is where engagements either build trust or accumulate quiet resentment. The differentiator is not how much you ship — it is how visibly you ship it. Reporting consistency in this phase is what earns the renewal conversation at QBR.

breakpoints: Account lead misunderstands level of client satisfaction/dissatisfaction
Slow delivery time on links or Reddit Ken work

milestones.name: QBR
milestones.description: Executive summary, goal vs. actual, channel breakdown, forward look.
milestones.narrative: End of each quarter. QBR structure: executive summary, goal vs. actual against Good/Better/Best scenarios, channel breakdown (organic, AEO, Reddit, conversion), wins tied to metric movement, misses with honest diagnosis, forward look on next quarter priorities, and strategic recommendations for expansion.
milestones.positioning: The QBR is also a sales motion, not just a reporting motion. The 'Forward look' and 'Strategic recommendations' sections are where renewals and expansions get teed up. A QBR that ends with 'here's what happened' without a 'here's what we should do next together' is a missed at-bat.

handoffs: QBR deck created by account lead, cross-functional partner inputs, send to Amy for approval and feedback. Team makes feedback, does walkthru, presents.
questions: Are we effectively managing upsell/cross-sell? How do we bake this in better?
breakpoints: QBR happens, but account lead doesn't document strategy changes per SOW
QBR doesn't happen because performance is down and they don't want to call attention to it
Misaligned expectations about what a successful QBR is - deliverables not at quality bar
